votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, correctly handling the output normalization, the conditions for when no even numbers exist, and the conditions for the minimum even value and its first occurrence index.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, correctly normalizing the output formats and faithfully implementing all conditions for both the empty/None case and the Some(v, i) case.

The Python code correctly normalizes the output and accurately translates all conditions of the Coq specification, including bounds checking, value matching, evenness, minimality, and first-occurrence index checking.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It handles the None case by checking no even values exist in arr, and for Some (v, i) it verifies: (1) i is a valid index with arr[i] = v, (2) v is even, (3) v is the smallest even value in arr, and (4) i is the first occurrence of v. The normalization of output (accepting None, [], or (v,i)/[v,i]) correctly maps to Coq's option type. The Python modulo check (val % 2 == 0) matches Coq's Nat.even for non-negative integers.

The normalization of output (accepting None, [], or (v,i)/[v,i]) correctly maps to Coq's option type. The Python modulo check (val % 2 == 0) matches Coq's Nat.even for non-negative integers.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It handles the None case by checking no even values exist in arr, and for Some (v, i) it verifies: (1) i is a valid index with arr[i] = v, (2) v is even, (3) v is the smallest even value in arr, and (4) i is the first occurrence of v. The normalization of output (accepting None, [], or (v,i)/[v,i]) correctly maps to Coq's option type. The Python modulo check for evenness matches Coq's Nat.even for non-negative integers.

Python enforces: None/[] iff no even numbers; Some(v,i) only if i in range, arr[i]=v, v even, v is the minimal even in arr, and i is the first index of v. This matches the Coq spec’s concrete conditions.

Python enforces: None/[] iff no even numbers; otherwise (v,i) must be in-bounds with arr[i]=v, v even, v minimal among evens, and i the first index of v. This matches the Coq spec on all non-negative integer inputs.

It matches the Coq spec: None only when no even elements; for Some(v,i) it checks i in bounds, arr[i]=v, v even, v minimal among evens, and i is first occurrence. Output shape normalization does not affect concrete behavior.
